fed_outlook.original_text: The Federal Reserve is widely expected to leave the federal funds rate unchanged at the 3.5%-3.75% target range in its January 2026 meeting, pausing its easing cycle after three consecutive rate cuts last year that pushed borrowing costs to their lowest level since 2022. In its December projections, the Fed signaled just one 25-basis-point rate cut in 2026. Markets currently expect that cut to come in June, while pricing in a smaller probability of an additional move in December.
key_factors: 트럼프 대통령의 정치적 압박 (Fed 독립성 이슈)
달러 약세 용인 발언으로 통화정책 압박 가중

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: The benchmark interest rate in the United States was last recorded at 3.75 percent.

sources.name: Trading Economics News
sources.original_text: Fed signaled just one 25-basis-point rate cut in 2026. Markets currently expect that cut to come in June.

sources.name: Barron's
sources.original_text: Traders see a 5% chance of a cut when the FOMC announces its interest-rate decision on Jan. 28. Odds of a quarter-point in cuts through June rose to 47.2%.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.original_text: Fed is likely to hold interest rates steady, resisting Trump pressure.

bok_outlook.original_text: The Bank of Korea unanimously kept its base rate unchanged at 2.5% for a fifth consecutive meeting in its first policy decision of 2026, in line with market expectations. The move reinforced signals that the current easing cycle has likely reached an end following a cumulative 100bps since October 2024, as policymakers shifted their focus toward financial stability with the won hovering near 16-year lows. In its latest statement, the BoK removed language on considering further cuts.

optimistic.description: 환율 안정, 하반기 경기 둔화 시 1회 인하

key_factors: 가계부채 및 수도권 부동산 가격 상승
반도체 중심 수출 호조 (성장 상방 리스크)

sources.name: Trading Economics
sources.original_text: The benchmark interest rate in South Korea was last recorded at 2.50 percent.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.original_text: BOK Keeps base rate unchanged at 2.50% as expected. BOK omits earlier language on plans to implement further cuts.

sources.name: Bank of Korea (Official)
sources.original_text: 금융통화위원회는 기준금리를 현재의 2.50% 수준에서 유지하여 통화정책을 운용하기로 하였다.

sources.name: 키움증권
sources.original_text: 한국은행은 통방문에서 '금리 인하 가능성을 열어두되', '기준금리의 추가 인하 여부 및 시기를 결정해 나갈 것'이라는 문구를 삭제하면서 인하 기대 자체를 완전히 축소시키는 결정을 내림.

usd_krw_short_term.rationale: 트럼프 달러 약세 용인으로 하방 압력, 그러나 한국 내부 불확실성(정치, 가계부채)으로 제한적

usd_krw_mid_term.rationale: 달러 약세 기조, WGBI 편입 효과, 경상수지 흑자 지속

fx_outlook.original_text: The US dollar sell-off intensified yesterday resulting in the dollar index breaking below last year's low at 96.218, and falling to a fresh low of 95.551. President Trump told reporters 'I think it's great' when asked if he was worried about the sell-off. The comments reinforce the impression amongst market participants that the Trump administration wants a weaker US dollar.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.original_text: Trump says 'I think it's great' about dollar weakness. Dollar worst one-day rout since April.

sources.name: MUFG Research
sources.original_text: USD sell-off accelerates after Trump welcomes weakness. Dollar index breaking below last year's low at 96.218, falling to 95.551.

sources.name: ING Think
sources.original_text: The dollar sold off across the board yesterday. Does the US administration just want a stronger yen or a broadly weaker dollar for competitive purposes?

sources.name: 아시아경제
sources.original_text: Won-Dollar Exchange Rate Expected to Peak in First Half and Ease in Second Half. Annual Average Projected at 1,400 Won in 2026.

year_end_forecast.impact: 금리차 축소 시 원화 강세 압력

hedge_recommendation.change_rationale: 트럼프 달러 약세 정책 명확화로 환노출 비중 상향 조정
rationale: 트럼프 대통령의 달러 약세 용인 발언으로 정책 방향 명확
DXY 4년래 최저, 달러 약세 모멘텀 강화
Fed-BOK 금리차 축소 전망 (Fed 인하 vs BOK 동결)
하반기 WGBI 편입 시 외국인 채권 투자 유입 예상
단, 한국 내부 정치/경제 불확실성으로 급격한 원화 강세는 제한적
risk_factors: 미중 무역갈등 격화 시 안전자산 선호로 달러 강세 반전 가능
한국 정치 불확실성 지속 시 원화 약세 압력
Fed 금리 인하 지연 시 달러 강세 가능
